file.content:
#!/bin/bash

# TaskAI Server Management Script
# Manages deployment, testing, health checks, and operations

set -e

# Colors for output
RED='\033[0;31m'
GREEN='\033[0;32m'
YELLOW='\033[1;33m'
BLUE='\033[0;34m'
PURPLE='\033[0;35m'
CYAN='\033[0;36m'
NC='\033[0m' # No Color

# Server connection
REMOTE_HOST="31.97.102.48"
REMOTE_USER="ubuntu"
REMOTE_SERVER="${REMOTE_USER}@${REMOTE_HOST}"

# Staging environment
STAGING_DEPLOY_PATH="/home/ubuntu/taskai-staging"
STAGING_DOMAIN="staging.taskai.cc"
STAGING_API_PORT=8082
STAGING_WEB_PORT=8086
STAGING_MCP_PORT=8084
STAGING_MCP_DOMAIN="mcp.staging.taskai.cc"

# Production environment
PROD_DEPLOY_PATH="/home/ubuntu/taskai"
PROD_DOMAIN="taskai.cc"
PROD_API_PORT=8081
PROD_WEB_PORT=8085
PROD_MCP_PORT=8083
PROD_MCP_DOMAIN="mcp.taskai.cc"

# Local development settings
LOCAL_WEB_PORT=8084
LOCAL_API_PORT=8083

# Resolve environment — sets REMOTE_DEPLOY_PATH, REMOTE_DOMAIN, REMOTE_API_PORT, REMOTE_WEB_PORT
resolve_env() {
    local env="${1:-staging}"
    case "$env" in
        prod|production)
            REMOTE_DEPLOY_PATH="$PROD_DEPLOY_PATH"
            REMOTE_DOMAIN="$PROD_DOMAIN"
            REMOTE_API_PORT="$PROD_API_PORT"
            REMOTE_WEB_PORT="$PROD_WEB_PORT"

}

# Function to print colored output
print_status() {
    echo -e "${GREEN}[INFO]${NC} $1"
}

print_warning() {
    echo -e "${YELLOW}[WARNING]${NC} $1"
}

print_error() {
    echo -e "${RED}[ERROR]${NC} $1"
}

print_success() {
    echo -e "${GREEN}[SUCCESS]${NC} $1"
}

print_header() {
    echo -e "${BLUE}=== $1 ===${NC}"
}

# Function to show help
show_help() {
    echo -e "${CYAN}TaskAI Server Management Script${NC}"
    echo ""
    echo "Usage: $0 <command> [options]"
    echo ""
    echo -e "${YELLOW}Local Development Commands:${NC}"

    echo "  local:clean          Stop and remove all local containers/volumes"

    echo -e "${YELLOW}Deployment Commands:${NC}"
    echo "  deploy [\"message\"]   Commit, push to main, auto-deploy to STAGING"
    echo "  promote [ref]        Promote to PRODUCTION (triggers GitHub Action)"
    echo ""
    echo -e "${YELLOW}Staging Commands (staging.taskai.cc):${NC}"
    echo "  staging status       Show staging service status"
    echo "  staging logs [svc]   Show staging logs"
    echo "  staging health       Check staging health"
    echo "  staging restart      Restart staging services"
    echo "  staging admin create <email>  Grant admin on staging"
    echo "  staging admin list            List admins on staging"
    echo "  staging admin revoke <email>  Revoke admin on staging"
    echo ""
    echo -e "${YELLOW}Production Commands (taskai.cc):${NC}"
    echo "  prod status          Show production service status"
    echo "  prod logs [svc]      Show production logs"
    echo "  prod health          Check production health"
    echo "  prod restart         Restart production services"
    echo "  prod admin create <email>    Grant admin on production"
    echo "  prod admin list              List admins on production"
    echo "  prod admin revoke <email>    Revoke admin on production"
    echo ""
    echo -e "${YELLOW}Test Commands:${NC}"
    echo "  test                 Run all tests (backend + frontend + coverage)"
    echo "  test-api             Run Go API tests"
    echo "  test-web             Run frontend tests"
    echo "  test-e2e             Run Playwright E2E tests"
    echo ""
    echo -e "${YELLOW}Admin Commands:${NC}"
    echo "  admin create <email> Create admin user (production)"
    echo "  admin list           List all admins (production)"
    echo "  admin revoke <email> Revoke admin privileges (production)"
    echo "  remote admin ...     Execute admin command on production"
    echo "  local admin ...      Execute admin command on local database"
    echo ""
    echo -e "${YELLOW}Database Commands:${NC}"
    echo "  db-migrate <src> <dst> [--yes]  Copy database between environments (DESTRUCTIVE)"
    echo "  db-query <query>                Execute SQL on production database"
    echo ""
    echo -e "${YELLOW}Utility Commands:${NC}"

file.content:
# Deploy to staging (commit, push, GitHub Actions handles staging deploy)
deploy_to_staging() {
    local commit_message="${1:-Deploy: automated deployment}"

    print_header "Deploying to Staging"

    # Check for uncommitted changes
    if [ -n "$(git status --porcelain)" ]; then
        print_status "Uncommitted changes detected, committing..."
        git add -A
        git commit -m "$commit_message

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>" || {
            print_error "Failed to commit changes"
            return 1
        }
    else
        print_status "No uncommitted changes found"
    fi

    # Push to remote
    print_status "Pushing to GitHub (triggers staging deploy)..."
    git push origin main || {
        print_error "Failed to push to GitHub"
        return 1
    }

    print_success "Code pushed to GitHub — staging deployment triggered"
    print_status "Monitor: https://github.com/anchoo2kewl/taskai/actions"
    echo ""

    # Wait and verify staging
    print_status "Waiting for staging deployment (60 seconds)..."
    sleep 60

    resolve_env staging
    print_status "Verifying staging deployment..."
    local max_attempts=20
    local attempt=1

    while [ $attempt -le $max_attempts ]; do
        if curl -f -s "https://$STAGING_DOMAIN/api/health" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
            print_success "Staging deployment successful!"
            echo ""
            echo -e "${CYAN}Staging URL:${NC} https://$STAGING_DOMAIN"
            echo -e "${CYAN}API Health:${NC} https://$STAGING_DOMAIN/api/health"
            echo ""
            print_status "To promote to production: $0 promote"
            return 0
        fi

        print_status "Waiting for staging to be ready (attempt $attempt/$max_attempts)..."
        sleep 5
        attempt=$((attempt + 1))
    done

    print_warning "Staging not responding yet — deployment may still be in progress"
    print_status "Check status: $0 staging health"
    print_status "Check actions: https://github.com/anchoo2kewl/taskai/actions"
    return 1
}

# Promote to production (triggers GitHub Actions workflow)
promote_to_production() {
    local ref="${1:-main}"

    print_header "Promoting to Production"
    echo -e "${CYAN}Ref:${NC} $ref"
    echo ""

    # Check if gh CLI is available
    if ! command -v gh &> /dev/null; then
        print_error "GitHub CLI (gh) is not installed"
        print_status "Install: brew install gh"
        return 1
    fi

    # Trigger the production deployment workflow
    print_status "Triggering production deployment..."
    gh workflow run deploy-production.yml \
        -f ref="$ref" \
        -f confirm="deploy" || {
        print_error "Failed to trigger production deployment"
        return 1
    }

    print_success "Production deployment triggered!"
    echo ""
    echo -e "${CYAN}Monitor:${NC} https://github.com/anchoo2kewl/taskai/actions"
    echo ""
    print_status "After deployment completes, verify: $0 prod health"
}

# Environment-specific operations
env_restart() {
    local env="$1"
    resolve_env "$env"
    print_header "Restarting $ENV_LABEL Services"

**Step 2: Rename server directories and DB files**

input.command:
ssh -o ConnectTimeout=10 ubuntu@31.97.102.48 << 'REMOTE_SCRIPT'
set -e

echo "-> Checking current state..."

OLD_STAGING="/home/ubuntu/sprintspark-staging"
NEW_STAGING="/home/ubuntu/taskai-staging"
OLD_PROD="/home/ubuntu/sprintspark"
NEW_PROD="/home/ubuntu/taskai"

# --- Staging ---
if [ -d "$OLD_STAGING" ]; then
    echo "-> Stopping staging containers..."
    cd "$OLD_STAGING" && docker compose down 2>/dev/null || true
    cd /home/ubuntu

    echo "-> Renaming staging directory..."
    mv "$OLD_STAGING" "$NEW_STAGING"
    echo "OK: $OLD_STAGING → $NEW_STAGING"
elif [ -d "$NEW_STAGING" ]; then
    echo "SKIP: $NEW_STAGING already exists"
else
    echo "WARN: Neither $OLD_STAGING nor $NEW_STAGING exists"
fi

# --- Production ---
if [ -d "$OLD_PROD" ]; then
    echo "-> Stopping production containers..."
    cd "$OLD_PROD" && docker compose down 2>/dev/null || true
    cd /home/ubuntu

    echo "-> Renaming production directory..."
    mv "$OLD_PROD" "$NEW_PROD"
    echo "OK: $OLD_PROD → $NEW_PROD"
elif [ -d "$NEW_PROD" ]; then
    echo "SKIP: $NEW_PROD already exists"
else
    echo "WARN: Neither $OLD_PROD nor $NEW_PROD exists"
fi

# --- Rename DB files inside data directories ---
for DIR in "$NEW_STAGING" "$NEW_PROD"; do
    if [ -d "$DIR/data" ]; then
        if [ -f "$DIR/data/sprintspark.db" ]; then
            echo "-> Renaming DB in $DIR/data..."
            mv "$DIR/data/sprintspark.db" "$DIR/data/taskai.db"
            [ -f "$DIR/data/sprintspark.db-shm" ] && mv "$DIR/data/sprintspark.db-shm" "$DIR/data/taskai.db-shm"
            [ -f "$DIR/data/sprintspark.db-wal" ] && mv "$DIR/data/sprintspark.db-wal" "$DIR/data/taskai.db-wal"
            echo "OK: DB renamed in $DIR"
        elif [ -f "$DIR/data/taskai.db" ]; then
            echo "SKIP: $DIR/data/taskai.db already exists"
        fi
    fi
done

# --- Also check Docker volumes for DB files ---
echo "-> Checking Docker volumes..."
for VOL in sprintspark-staging-data sprintspark-data; do
    MOUNT=$(docker volume inspect "$VOL" --format '{{ .Mountpoint }}' 2>/dev/null || echo "")
    if [ -n "$MOUNT" ] && [ -f "$MOUNT/sprintspark.db" ]; then
        echo "-> Renaming DB in Docker volume $VOL..."
        sudo mv "$MOUNT/sprintspark.db" "$MOUNT/taskai.db"
        [ -f "$MOUNT/sprintspark.db-shm" ] && sudo mv "$MOUNT/sprintspark.db-shm" "$MOUNT/taskai.db-shm"
        [ -f "$MOUNT/sprintspark.db-wal" ] && sudo mv "$MOUNT/sprintspark.db-wal" "$MOUNT/taskai.db-wal"
        echo "OK: DB renamed in volume $VOL"
    fi
done

echo "-> Directory and DB renames complete"
REMOTE_SCRIPT
input.description: Step 2: Rename server directories and DB files

content.thinking:
Hmm, `staging.taskai.cc` resolves to `129.213.82.37` which is different from the server IP `31.97.102.48`. It's possible the DNS records were created with Cloudflare proxy enabled (orange cloud). And `mcp.staging.taskai.cc` appears to be a CNAME to `taskai.cc` which then resolves to `31.97.102.48`.

Wait, the staging DNS resolves to `129.213.82.37` - that's a Cloudflare proxy IP. The user likely created the records with proxy enabled. Let me check if certbot can work with this, or if the user needs to turn off the proxy (gray cloud) for the SSL cert to work.
 
If Cloudflare's proxy is enabled, the HTTP challenge will fail because requests route through Cloudflare instead of hitting our server directly. The user needs to disable the proxy for those DNS records so Let's Encrypt can validate them, or switch to Cloudflare's own SSL. Let me verify whether the MCP staging record is also proxied. "Full (Strict)" SSL mode with Cloudflare's own SSL on the edge, and either a self-signed cert or Cloudflare Origin Certificate on the server. The simplest approach for the migration is to turn off the proxy (DNS only / gray cloud). Actually, let me just try running certbot—if Cloudflare is in "Full" mode, it should forward HTTP requests on port 80 to nginx, which means the ACME challenge might work even with the proxy enabled.

Though there's a risk that Cloudflare redirects HTTP to HTTPS automatically, which would break the challenge. I'll just attempt it and see what happens.

content.text: `staging.taskai.cc` resolves to `129.213.82.37` (Cloudflare proxy IP). That means the records were created with the orange cloud (proxy) enabled. Certbot HTTP challenge may fail through Cloudflare's proxy. Let me try anyway - if it fails, we'll need the proxy turned off.

input.command:
ssh -o ConnectTimeout=10 ubuntu@31.97.102.48 << 'REMOTE_SCRIPT'
set -e

echo "=== Step 5: SSL Certificates ==="

for DOMAIN in staging.taskai.cc mcp.staging.taskai.cc; do
    if [ -f "/etc/letsencrypt/live/$DOMAIN/fullchain.pem" ]; then
        echo "SKIP: SSL cert already exists for $DOMAIN"
    else
        echo "-> Obtaining SSL cert for $DOMAIN..."
        sudo certbot --nginx -d "$DOMAIN" \
            --non-interactive --agree-tos \
            --email anshuman@biswas.me \
            --redirect 2>&1 || echo "FAIL: SSL cert failed for $DOMAIN"
    fi
done
REMOTE_SCRIPT

ssh -o ConnectTimeout=10 ubuntu@31.97.102.48 << 'REMOTE_SCRIPT'
set -e

echo "=== Checking docker-compose files for old paths ==="

for DIR in /home/ubuntu/taskai-staging /home/ubuntu/taskai; do
    if [ -f "$DIR/docker-compose.yml" ]; then
        echo "--- $DIR/docker-compose.yml ---"
        grep -n "sprintspark" "$DIR/docker-compose.yml" || echo "(no old references)"
        echo ""
    fi
done

echo "=== Checking source directories ==="
for DIR in /home/ubuntu/taskai-staging /home/ubuntu/taskai; do
    echo "--- $DIR ---"
    ls -la "$DIR/" 2>/dev/null || echo "DIR NOT FOUND"
    echo ""
    if [ -d "$DIR/source/.git" ]; then
        cd "$DIR/source"
        echo "Git branch: $(git branch --show-current)"
        echo "Git status (short):"
        git status -s | head -20
        echo ""
    fi
done

file.content:
services:
  api:
    build:
      context: ./api
      dockerfile: Dockerfile
    ports:
      - "8083:8080"
    environment:
      - DB_PATH=/data/taskai.db
      - JWT_SECRET=${JWT_SECRET:-dev-secret-change-in-production}
      - PORT=8080
      - ENV=${ENV:-production}
      - CORS_ALLOWED_ORIGINS=${CORS_ALLOWED_ORIGINS:-https://taskai.cc,https://staging.taskai.cc}
      - LOG_LEVEL=${LOG_LEVEL:-info}
    volumes:
      # Mount SQLite database for persistence
      - taskai-data:/data
    networks:
      - taskai
    restart: unless-stopped
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD", "wget", "--no-verbose", "--tries=1", "--spider", "http://localhost:8080/api/health"]
      interval: 30s
      timeout: 3s
      retries: 3
      start_period: 5s

  web:
    build:
      context: .
      dockerfile: ./web/Dockerfile
    ports:
      - "8084:80"
    networks:
      - taskai
    depends_on:
      api:
        condition: service_healthy
    restart: unless-stopped
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD", "curl", "-f", "http://localhost/health"]
      interval: 30s
      timeout: 3s
      retries: 3
      start_period: 5s

  mcp:
    build:
      context: ./mcp
      dockerfile: Dockerfile
    ports:
      - "8089:3000"
    environment:
      - TASKAI_API_URL=http://api:8080
      - PORT=3000
    networks:
      - taskai
    depends_on:
      api:
        condition: service_healthy
    restart: unless-stopped
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD", "wget", "--no-verbose", "--tries=1", "--spider", "http://localhost:3000/health"]
      interval: 30s
      timeout: 3s
      retries: 3
      start_period: 5s

volumes:
  # Persistent volume for SQLite database
  taskai-data:
    driver: local

networks:
  taskai:
    driver: bridge

content.text: Now let me fix the server - reset git repos and update docker-compose files with new paths/names.

input.command:
ssh -o ConnectTimeout=10 ubuntu@31.97.102.48 << 'REMOTE_SCRIPT'
set -e

echo "=== Fixing git repos - hard reset to latest ==="

for DIR in /home/ubuntu/taskai-staging/source /home/ubuntu/taskai/source; do
    if [ -d "$DIR/.git" ]; then
        echo "-> Resetting $DIR..."
        cd "$DIR"
        git fetch origin main
        git reset --hard origin/main
        git clean -fd
        echo "OK: $DIR reset to $(git log --oneline -1)"
        echo ""
    fi
done

echo "=== Updating docker-compose.yml files ==="

# --- Staging docker-compose.yml ---
echo "-> Updating staging docker-compose.yml..."
cat > /home/ubuntu/taskai-staging/docker-compose.yml << 'COMPOSE'
services:
  api:
    build:
      context: /home/ubuntu/taskai-staging/source/api
      dockerfile: Dockerfile
    ports:
      - "8082:8080"
    environment:
      - DB_PATH=/data/taskai.db
      - JWT_SECRET=${JWT_SECRET}
      - PORT=8080
      - ENV=staging
      - CORS_ALLOWED_ORIGINS=https://staging.taskai.cc
      - LOG_LEVEL=${LOG_LEVEL:-info}
      - BREVO_API_KEY=${BREVO_API_KEY:-}
      - CLOUDINARY_CLOUD_NAME=${CLOUDINARY_CLOUD_NAME:-}
      - CLOUDINARY_API_KEY=${CLOUDINARY_API_KEY:-}
      - CLOUDINARY_API_SECRET=${CLOUDINARY_API_SECRET:-}
    volumes:
      - taskai-staging-data:/data
    networks:
      - taskai-staging-network
    restart: unless-stopped
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD", "wget", "--no-verbose", "--tries=1", "--spider", "http://localhost:8080/api/health"]
      interval: 30s
      timeout: 3s
      retries: 3
      start_period: 5s

  web:
    build:
      context: /home/ubuntu/taskai-staging/source
      dockerfile: ./web/Dockerfile
    ports:
      - "8086:80"
    container_name: taskai-staging-web
    networks:
      - taskai-staging-network
    depends_on:
      api:
        condition: service_healthy
    restart: unless-stopped
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD", "curl", "-f", "http://localhost/health"]
      interval: 30s
      timeout: 3s
      retries: 3
      start_period: 5s

  mcp:
    build:
      context: /home/ubuntu/taskai-staging/source/mcp
      dockerfile: Dockerfile
    ports:
      - "8084:3000"
    container_name: taskai-staging-mcp
    environment:
      - TASKAI_API_URL=http://api:8080
      - PORT=3000
    networks:
      - taskai-staging-network
    depends_on:
      api:
        condition: service_healthy
    restart: unless-stopped
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD", "wget", "--no-verbose", "--tries=1", "--spider", "http://localhost:3000/health"]
      interval: 30s
      timeout: 3s
      retries: 3
      start_period: 5s

volumes:
  taskai-staging-data:
    driver: local

networks:
  taskai-staging-network:
    driver: bridge
COMPOSE
echo "OK: staging docker-compose.yml updated"

# --- Production docker-compose.yml ---
echo "-> Updating production docker-compose.yml..."
cat > /home/ubuntu/taskai/docker-compose.yml << 'COMPOSE'
services:
  api:
    build:
      context: /home/ubuntu/taskai/source/api
      dockerfile: Dockerfile
    ports:
      - "8081:8080"
    environment:
      - DB_PATH=/data/taskai.db
      - JWT_SECRET=${JWT_SECRET}
      - PORT=8080
      - ENV=production
      - CORS_ALLOWED_ORIGINS=https://taskai.cc
      - LOG_LEVEL=${LOG_LEVEL:-info}
      - BREVO_API_KEY=${BREVO_API_KEY:-}
      - CLOUDINARY_CLOUD_NAME=${CLOUDINARY_CLOUD_NAME:-}
      - CLOUDINARY_API_KEY=${CLOUDINARY_API_KEY:-}
      - CLOUDINARY_API_SECRET=${CLOUDINARY_API_SECRET:-}
    volumes:
      - taskai-data:/data
    networks:
      - taskai-network
    restart: unless-stopped
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD", "wget", "--no-verbose", "--tries=1", "--spider", "http://localhost:8080/api/health"]
      interval: 30s
      timeout: 3s
      retries: 3
      start_period: 5s

  web:
    build:
      context: /home/ubuntu/taskai/source
      dockerfile: ./web/Dockerfile
    ports:
      - "8085:80"
    container_name: taskai-web
    networks:
      - taskai-network
    depends_on:
      api:
        condition: service_healthy
    restart: unless-stopped
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD", "curl", "-f", "http://localhost/health"]
      interval: 30s
      timeout: 3s
      retries: 3
      start_period: 5s

  mcp:
    build:
      context: /home/ubuntu/taskai/source/mcp
      dockerfile: Dockerfile
    ports:
      - "8083:3000"
    container_name: taskai-mcp
    environment:
      - TASKAI_API_URL=http://api:8080
      - PORT=3000
    networks:
      - taskai-network
    depends_on:
      api:
        condition: service_healthy
    restart: unless-stopped
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD", "wget", "--no-verbose", "--tries=1", "--spider", "http://localhost:3000/health"]
      interval: 30s
      timeout: 3s
      retries: 3
      start_period: 5s

volumes:
  taskai-data:
    driver: local

networks:
  taskai-network:
    driver: bridge
COMPOSE
echo "OK: production docker-compose.yml updated"

echo ""
echo "=== Preserving env files ==="
for DIR in /home/ubuntu/taskai-staging /home/ubuntu/taskai; do
    if [ -f "$DIR/.env" ]; then
        echo "OK: $DIR/.env exists"
    else
        echo "WARN: No .env in $DIR"
    fi
done
REMOTE_SCRIPT

#!/bin/bash
# Deployment script for SprintSpark (production)
# This script rebuilds and restarts the Docker containers

set -e

APP_DIR="/home/ubuntu/sprintspark"
cd "$APP_DIR"

echo "=== Deploying SprintSpark (production) ==="

echo "-> Building Docker images..."
docker compose build --no-cache

echo "-> Stopping old containers..."
docker compose down

echo "-> Starting new containers..."
docker compose up -d

echo "-> Waiting for services to be healthy..."
sleep 10

# Check API health
echo "-> Checking API health..."
for i in {1..30}; do
    if curl -sf http://127.0.0.1:8081/api/health > /dev/null 2>&1; then
        echo "OK: API is healthy"
        break
    fi
    if [ $i -eq 30 ]; then
        echo "FAIL: API health check failed"
        docker compose logs api | tail -20
        exit 1
    fi
    sleep 2
done

# Check web health
echo "-> Checking web health..."
for i in {1..30}; do
    if curl -sf http://127.0.0.1:8085/health > /dev/null 2>&1; then
        echo "OK: Web is healthy"
        break
    fi
    if [ $i -eq 30 ]; then
        echo "FAIL: Web health check failed"
        docker compose logs web | tail -20
        exit 1
    fi
    sleep 2
done

echo "-> Cleaning up old Docker images..."
docker image prune -f

echo ""
echo "=== Deployment completed successfully! ==="
echo ""
echo "Environment: production"
echo "URL: https://taskai.cc"
echo ""
echo "Check logs: docker compose logs -f"
---

echo "-> Building Docker images..."
docker compose build --no-cache

echo "-> Stopping old containers..."
docker compose down

echo "-> Starting new containers..."
docker compose up -d

echo "-> Waiting for services to be healthy..."
sleep 10

# Check API health
echo "-> Checking API health..."
for i in {1..30}; do
    if curl -sf http://127.0.0.1:8082/api/health > /dev/null 2>&1; then
        echo "OK: API is healthy"
        break
    fi
    if [ $i -eq 30 ]; then
        echo "FAIL: API health check failed"
        docker compose logs api | tail -20
        exit 1
    fi
    sleep 2
done

# Check web health
echo "-> Checking web health..."
for i in {1..30}; do
    if curl -sf http://127.0.0.1:8086/health > /dev/null 2>&1; then
        echo "OK: Web is healthy"
        break
    fi
    if [ $i -eq 30 ]; then
        echo "FAIL: Web health check failed"
        docker compose logs web | tail -20
        exit 1
    fi
    sleep 2
done

echo "-> Cleaning up old Docker images..."
docker image prune -f

echo ""
echo "=== Deployment completed successfully! ==="

echo "Environment: staging"
echo "URL: https://sprintspark.biswas.me"
echo ""

Good. The webhook config is already updated with the new `taskai-staging` name and path. The old Docker volumes are `sprintspark-staging_sprintspark-staging-data` and `sprintspark_sprintspark-data` (note the project prefix from docker compose).

For env vars - there are no `.env` files at the docker-compose level for either environment. The JWT_SECRET must have been set some other way - perhaps the old docker-compose had it hardcoded, or there was an env file that got lost.

Let me look at the web .env files (which exist) and the api .env.example to understand what's needed.Actually, the critical thing is: without a JWT_SECRET, the app will use the default from docker-compose which would be empty (since it's `${JWT_SECRET}` without a default). This would break authentication.Let me check the old docker-compose files I saw earlier for the JWT_SECRET value. Looking back at the output:


The old staging compose had the same `JWT_SECRET=${JWT_SECRET}` pattern, so there must have been an .env file or it was passed at runtime. Now I'm realizing the volume name mismatch - the old volumes use the `sprintspark` project prefix while the new compose references `taskai-staging` and `taskai`, which means the data won't carry over unless the volumes are explicitly mapped or renamed. So the old production setup used a named volume `sprintspark-data` that Docker prefixed with the project name, and we renamed the database file itself from `sprintspark.db` to `taskai.db` in step 2. The question is whether the `/home/ubuntu/taskai/data/` directory on the host was a bind mount for backups or if it served a different purpose than the Docker volume being used by the running container.

I need to determine which storage mechanism the containers are actually reading from, make sure the new setup can access the database, and track down the JWT_SECRET value.

content.thinking:
Now I have a clear picture:

1. **Database**: The real databases are in Docker volumes:
   - Staging: `sprintspark-staging_sprintspark-staging-data` → contains `sprintspark.db` (still old name!)
   - Production: `sprintspark_sprintspark-data` → contains `sprintspark.db` (still old name!)
   - The data directories at `/home/ubuntu/taskai/data/` are separate and contain either empty or old files

2. **Volumes**: The new docker-compose references `taskai-staging-data` and `taskai-data`, which when run from directories `taskai-staging` and `taskai`, will create volumes named `taskai-staging_taskai-staging-data` and `taskai_taskai-data`. These are NEW volumes that won't have the existing data.


3. **JWT_SECRET**: Not found anywhere. The old containers must have been started with it somehow. Since the old containers are stopped and gone, I can't inspect them. For the database, I could either reference the old volumes as external in the new docker-compose or copy the data out to bind mounts, but I'll need to rename the database files from `sprintspark.db` to `taskai.db` either way.

I think the cleanest path forward is using external volumes with the old names and renaming the DB files inside them—that's the least disruptive. For the JWT_SECRET, since existing passwords are hashed with bcrypt and don't depend on it, I can generate a new one without breaking authentication. Users would just need to log in again after the migration, which is acceptable.

So my plan is to rename the DB files in the Docker volumes, update docker-compose to reference those external volumes, generate a new JWT_SECRET for the .env files, then build and start everything.

content.text:
Now I see the full picture. Key issues:

1. **DB lives in Docker volumes** (`sprintspark-staging_sprintspark-staging-data` and `sprintspark_sprintspark-data`) with files still named `sprintspark.db`
2. **New docker-compose will create new empty volumes** - we need to reference the old ones
3. **No JWT_SECRET** found - need to set one (existing users just need to re-login)

Let me fix all of this.

input.command:
ssh -o ConnectTimeout=10 ubuntu@31.97.102.48 << 'REMOTE_SCRIPT'
set -e

echo "=== Step 1: Rename DB files inside Docker volumes ==="

# Staging volume
STAGING_MOUNT=$(docker volume inspect "sprintspark-staging_sprintspark-staging-data" --format '{{ .Mountpoint }}' 2>/dev/null || echo "")
if [ -n "$STAGING_MOUNT" ] && [ -f "$STAGING_MOUNT/sprintspark.db" ]; then
    echo "-> Renaming staging DB..."
    sudo mv "$STAGING_MOUNT/sprintspark.db" "$STAGING_MOUNT/taskai.db"
    [ -f "$STAGING_MOUNT/sprintspark.db-shm" ] && sudo mv "$STAGING_MOUNT/sprintspark.db-shm" "$STAGING_MOUNT/taskai.db-shm"
    [ -f "$STAGING_MOUNT/sprintspark.db-wal" ] && sudo mv "$STAGING_MOUNT/sprintspark.db-wal" "$STAGING_MOUNT/taskai.db-wal"
    echo "OK: Staging DB renamed"
    sudo ls -la "$STAGING_MOUNT/"
else
    echo "WARN: Staging volume DB not found or already renamed"
fi

echo ""

# Production volume
PROD_MOUNT=$(docker volume inspect "sprintspark_sprintspark-data" --format '{{ .Mountpoint }}' 2>/dev/null || echo "")
if [ -n "$PROD_MOUNT" ] && [ -f "$PROD_MOUNT/sprintspark.db" ]; then
    echo "-> Renaming production DB..."
    sudo mv "$PROD_MOUNT/sprintspark.db" "$PROD_MOUNT/taskai.db"
    [ -f "$PROD_MOUNT/sprintspark.db-shm" ] && sudo mv "$PROD_MOUNT/sprintspark.db-shm" "$PROD_MOUNT/taskai.db-shm"
    [ -f "$PROD_MOUNT/sprintspark.db-wal" ] && sudo mv "$PROD_MOUNT/sprintspark.db-wal" "$PROD_MOUNT/taskai.db-wal"
    echo "OK: Production DB renamed"
    sudo ls -la "$PROD_MOUNT/"
else
    echo "WARN: Production volume DB not found or already renamed"
fi

echo ""
echo "=== Step 2: Generate JWT_SECRET and create .env files ==="

JWT_SECRET=$(openssl rand -hex 32)

# Staging .env
cat > /home/ubuntu/taskai-staging/.env << EOF
JWT_SECRET=${JWT_SECRET}
EOF
echo "OK: Staging .env created"

# Production .env (same secret so users can use either)
cat > /home/ubuntu/taskai/.env << EOF
JWT_SECRET=${JWT_SECRET}
EOF
echo "OK: Production .env created"

echo ""
echo "=== Step 3: Update docker-compose to use external volumes ==="

# Staging
cat > /home/ubuntu/taskai-staging/docker-compose.yml << 'COMPOSE'
services:
  api:
    build:
      context: /home/ubuntu/taskai-staging/source/api
      dockerfile: Dockerfile
    ports:
      - "8082:8080"
    environment:
      - DB_PATH=/data/taskai.db
      - JWT_SECRET=${JWT_SECRET}
      - PORT=8080
      - ENV=staging
      - CORS_ALLOWED_ORIGINS=https://staging.taskai.cc
      - LOG_LEVEL=${LOG_LEVEL:-info}
      - BREVO_API_KEY=${BREVO_API_KEY:-}
      - CLOUDINARY_CLOUD_NAME=${CLOUDINARY_CLOUD_NAME:-}
      - CLOUDINARY_API_KEY=${CLOUDINARY_API_KEY:-}
      - CLOUDINARY_API_SECRET=${CLOUDINARY_API_SECRET:-}
    volumes:
      - staging-data:/data
    networks:
      - taskai-staging-network
    restart: unless-stopped
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD", "wget", "--no-verbose", "--tries=1", "--spider", "http://localhost:8080/api/health"]
      interval: 30s
      timeout: 3s
      retries: 3
      start_period: 5s

  web:
    build:
      context: /home/ubuntu/taskai-staging/source
      dockerfile: ./web/Dockerfile
    ports:
      - "8086:80"
    container_name: taskai-staging-web
    networks:
      - taskai-staging-network
    depends_on:
      api:
        condition: service_healthy
    restart: unless-stopped
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD", "curl", "-f", "http://localhost/health"]
      interval: 30s
      timeout: 3s
      retries: 3
      start_period: 5s

  mcp:
    build:
      context: /home/ubuntu/taskai-staging/source/mcp
      dockerfile: Dockerfile
    ports:
      - "8084:3000"
    container_name: taskai-staging-mcp
    environment:
      - TASKAI_API_URL=http://api:8080
      - PORT=3000
    networks:
      - taskai-staging-network
    depends_on:
      api:
        condition: service_healthy
    restart: unless-stopped
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD", "wget", "--no-verbose", "--tries=1", "--spider", "http://localhost:3000/health"]
      interval: 30s
      timeout: 3s
      retries: 3
      start_period: 5s

volumes:
  staging-data:
    external: true
    name: sprintspark-staging_sprintspark-staging-data

networks:
  taskai-staging-network:
    driver: bridge
COMPOSE
echo "OK: Staging docker-compose updated with external volume"

# Production
cat > /home/ubuntu/taskai/docker-compose.yml << 'COMPOSE'
services:
  api:
    build:
      context: /home/ubuntu/taskai/source/api
      dockerfile: Dockerfile
    ports:
      - "8081:8080"
    environment:
      - DB_PATH=/data/taskai.db
      - JWT_SECRET=${JWT_SECRET}
      - PORT=8080
      - ENV=production
      - CORS_ALLOWED_ORIGINS=https://taskai.cc
      - LOG_LEVEL=${LOG_LEVEL:-info}
      - BREVO_API_KEY=${BREVO_API_KEY:-}
      - CLOUDINARY_CLOUD_NAME=${CLOUDINARY_CLOUD_NAME:-}
      - CLOUDINARY_API_KEY=${CLOUDINARY_API_KEY:-}
      - CLOUDINARY_API_SECRET=${CLOUDINARY_API_SECRET:-}
    volumes:
      - prod-data:/data
    networks:
      - taskai-network
    restart: unless-stopped
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD", "wget", "--no-verbose", "--tries=1", "--spider", "http://localhost:8080/api/health"]
      interval: 30s
      timeout: 3s
      retries: 3
      start_period: 5s

  web:
    build:
      context: /home/ubuntu/taskai/source
      dockerfile: ./web/Dockerfile
    ports:
      - "8085:80"
    container_name: taskai-web
    networks:
      - taskai-network
    depends_on:
      api:
        condition: service_healthy
    restart: unless-stopped
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD", "curl", "-f", "http://localhost/health"]
      interval: 30s
      timeout: 3s
      retries: 3
      start_period: 5s

  mcp:
    build:
      context: /home/ubuntu/taskai/source/mcp
      dockerfile: Dockerfile
    ports:
      - "8083:3000"
    container_name: taskai-mcp
    environment:
      - TASKAI_API_URL=http://api:8080
      - PORT=3000
    networks:
      - taskai-network
    depends_on:
      api:
        condition: service_healthy
    restart: unless-stopped
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD", "wget", "--no-verbose", "--tries=1", "--spider", "http://localhost:3000/health"]
      interval: 30s
      timeout: 3s
      retries: 3
      start_period: 5s

volumes:
  prod-data:
    external: true
    name: sprintspark_sprintspark-data

networks:
  taskai-network:
    driver: bridge
COMPOSE
echo "OK: Production docker-compose updated with external volume"
REMOTE_SCRIPT
input.description: Rename DBs in volumes, create .env, update compose with external volumes

content.text: Production is up and healthy! Now staging.

input.command:
ssh -o ConnectTimeout=10 ubuntu@31.97.102.48 << 'REMOTE_SCRIPT'
set -e

echo "=== Building and starting STAGING ==="
cd /home/ubuntu/taskai-staging
echo "-> Building..."
docker compose build --no-cache 2>&1 | tail -5
echo "-> Starting..."
docker compose up -d 2>&1
echo "-> Waiting for health..."
sleep 15

for i in $(seq 1 20); do
    if curl -sf http://127.0.0.1:8082/api/health > /dev/null 2>&1; then
        echo "OK: Staging API is healthy!"
        break
    fi
    if [ $i -eq 20 ]; then
        echo "FAIL: Staging API health check failed"
        docker compose logs api 2>&1 | tail -20
    fi
    sleep 3
done

echo ""
echo "=== Staging container status ==="
docker compose ps 2>&1
REMOTE_SCRIPT
input.description: Build and start staging

content.text: Both environments are up and healthy. Now let me handle the SSL cert for `staging.taskai.cc` (needs Cloudflare proxy off), update deploy scripts, set up GitHub secrets, and run final health checks.

input.command: dig +short staging.taskai.cc A @8.8.8.8
input.description: Check if staging DNS proxy is off yet

content.text:
`staging.taskai.cc` still resolves to Cloudflare proxy IP (`129.213.82.37`). **You need to turn off the orange cloud (proxy) for `staging.taskai.cc`** in Cloudflare so certbot can issue the SSL cert. Set it to "DNS only" (gray cloud).

While we wait for that, let me update the deploy scripts and GitHub secrets.

input.command:
ssh -o ConnectTimeout=10 ubuntu@31.97.102.48 << 'REMOTE_SCRIPT'
set -e

echo "=== Updating deploy scripts ==="

# Staging deploy script
cat > /home/ubuntu/taskai-staging/deploy-app.sh << 'SCRIPT'
#!/bin/bash
# Deployment script for TaskAI (staging)
set -e
APP_DIR="/home/ubuntu/taskai-staging"
cd "$APP_DIR"
echo "=== Deploying TaskAI (staging) ==="
echo "-> Building Docker images..."
docker compose build --no-cache
echo "-> Stopping old containers..."
docker compose down
echo "-> Starting new containers..."
docker compose up -d
echo "-> Waiting for services to be healthy..."
sleep 10
echo "-> Checking API health..."
for i in {1..30}; do
    if curl -sf http://127.0.0.1:8082/api/health > /dev/null 2>&1; then
        echo "OK: API is healthy"
        break
    fi
    if [ $i -eq 30 ]; then
        echo "FAIL: API health check failed"
        docker compose logs api | tail -20
        exit 1
    fi
    sleep 2
done
echo "-> Checking web health..."
for i in {1..30}; do
    if curl -sf http://127.0.0.1:8086/health > /dev/null 2>&1; then
        echo "OK: Web is healthy"
        break
    fi
    if [ $i -eq 30 ]; then
        echo "FAIL: Web health check failed"
        docker compose logs web | tail -20
        exit 1
    fi
    sleep 2
done
echo "-> Cleaning up old Docker images..."
docker image prune -f
echo ""
echo "=== Deployment completed successfully! ==="
echo "Environment: staging"
echo "URL: https://staging.taskai.cc"
SCRIPT
chmod +x /home/ubuntu/taskai-staging/deploy-app.sh
echo "OK: Staging deploy script updated"

# Production deploy script
cat > /home/ubuntu/taskai/deploy-app.sh << 'SCRIPT'
#!/bin/bash
# Deployment script for TaskAI (production)
set -e
APP_DIR="/home/ubuntu/taskai"
cd "$APP_DIR"
echo "=== Deploying TaskAI (production) ==="
echo "-> Building Docker images..."
docker compose build --no-cache
echo "-> Stopping old containers..."
docker compose down
echo "-> Starting new containers..."
docker compose up -d
echo "-> Waiting for services to be healthy..."
sleep 10
echo "-> Checking API health..."
for i in {1..30}; do
    if curl -sf http://127.0.0.1:8081/api/health > /dev/null 2>&1; then
        echo "OK: API is healthy"
        break
    fi
    if [ $i -eq 30 ]; then
        echo "FAIL: API health check failed"
        docker compose logs api | tail -20
        exit 1
    fi
    sleep 2
done
echo "-> Checking web health..."
for i in {1..30}; do
    if curl -sf http://127.0.0.1:8085/health > /dev/null 2>&1; then
        echo "OK: Web is healthy"
        break
    fi
    if [ $i -eq 30 ]; then
        echo "FAIL: Web health check failed"
        docker compose logs web | tail -20
        exit 1
    fi
    sleep 2
done
echo "-> Cleaning up old Docker images..."
docker image prune -f
echo ""
echo "=== Deployment completed successfully! ==="
echo "Environment: production"
echo "URL: https://taskai.cc"
SCRIPT
chmod +x /home/ubuntu/taskai/deploy-app.sh
echo "OK: Production deploy script updated"
REMOTE_SCRIPT

content.text: Production is fully healthy. Let me check the staging deploy workflow to make sure the webhook URL is correct.

content.content:
1→name: Deploy to Staging
     2→
     3→on:
     4→  workflow_run:
     5→    workflows: ["CI"]
     6→    types: [completed]
     7→    branches: [main]
     8→
     9→  # Allow manual trigger from GitHub UI
    10→  workflow_dispatch:
    11→
    12→jobs:
    13→  deploy:
    14→    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    15→    timeout-minutes: 15
    16→    environment: staging
    17→    if: ${{ github.event_name == 'workflow_dispatch' || github.event.workflow_run.conclusion == 'success' }}
    18→
    19→    steps:
    20→      - name: Trigger staging webhook
    21→        run: |
    22→          echo "=== Deploying TaskAI to STAGING ==="
    23→          RESPONSE=$(curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" \
    24→            -X POST "https://webhook.biswas.me/webhook/taskai-staging" \
    25→            -H "X-Hub-Signature-256: sha256=$(echo -n '{}' | openssl dgst -sha256 -hmac '${{ secrets.STAGING_WEBHOOK_SECRET }}' | awk '{print $2}')" \
    26→            -H "X-GitHub-Event: push" \
    27→            -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
    28→            -d '{}')
    29→          echo "Webhook response: $RESPONSE"
    30→          if [ "$RESPONSE" -lt 200 ] || [ "$RESPONSE" -ge 300 ]; then
    31→            echo "WARNING: Webhook returned $RESPONSE, deployment may not have triggered"
    32→          fi
    33→
    34→      - name: Wait for deployment
    35→        run: |
    36→          echo "Waiting 60 seconds for deployment to complete..."
    37→          sleep 60
    38→
    39→      - name: Verify staging health
    40→        run: |
    41→          echo "-> Checking API health..."
    42→          for i in $(seq 1 30); do
    43→            if curl -sf https://staging.taskai.cc/api/health > /dev/null 2>&1; then

file.content:
name: Deploy to Staging

on:
  workflow_run:
    workflows: ["CI"]
    types: [completed]
    branches: [main]

  # Allow manual trigger from GitHub UI
  workflow_dispatch:

jobs:
  deploy:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    timeout-minutes: 15
    environment: staging
    if: ${{ github.event_name == 'workflow_dispatch' || github.event.workflow_run.conclusion == 'success' }}

    steps:
      - name: Trigger staging webhook
        run: |
          echo "=== Deploying TaskAI to STAGING ==="
          RESPONSE=$(curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" \
            -X POST "https://webhook.biswas.me/webhook/taskai-staging" \
            -H "X-Hub-Signature-256: sha256=$(echo -n '{}' | openssl dgst -sha256 -hmac '${{ secrets.STAGING_WEBHOOK_SECRET }}' | awk '{print $2}')" \
            -H "X-GitHub-Event: push" \
            -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
            -d '{}')
          echo "Webhook response: $RESPONSE"
          if [ "$RESPONSE" -lt 200 ] || [ "$RESPONSE" -ge 300 ]; then
            echo "WARNING: Webhook returned $RESPONSE, deployment may not have triggered"
          fi

      - name: Wait for deployment
        run: |
          echo "Waiting 60 seconds for deployment to complete..."
          sleep 60

      - name: Verify staging health
        run: |
          echo "-> Checking API health..."
          for i in $(seq 1 30); do
            if curl -sf https://staging.taskai.cc/api/health > /dev/null 2>&1; then
              echo "OK: API is healthy"
              break
            fi
            if [ $i -eq 30 ]; then
              echo "FAIL: API health check failed"
              exit 1
            fi
            sleep 5
          done

          echo "-> Checking web health..."
          for i in $(seq 1 30); do
            if curl -sf -o /dev/null https://staging.taskai.cc/ 2>&1; then
              echo "OK: Web is healthy"
              break
            fi
            if [ $i -eq 30 ]; then
              echo "FAIL: Web health check failed"
              exit 1
            fi
            sleep 5
          done

          echo "-> Checking MCP health..."
          for i in $(seq 1 10); do
            if curl -sf https://mcp.staging.taskai.cc/health > /dev/null 2>&1; then
              echo "OK: MCP is healthy"
              break
            fi
            if [ $i -eq 10 ]; then
              echo "WARNING: MCP health check failed (non-blocking)"
            fi
            sleep 3
          done

          echo ""
          echo "=== Staging deployment verified! ==="
          echo "URL: https://staging.taskai.cc"

content.text: Good - the new staging server has Docker, Docker Compose, nginx, and certbot. Now let me set up staging there.

input.command:
ssh -o ConnectTimeout=10 ubuntu@129.213.82.37 << 'REMOTE_SCRIPT'
set -e

echo "=== Setting up TaskAI staging ==="

# Create directory structure
mkdir -p /home/ubuntu/taskai-staging/{data,logs,source}
echo "OK: Directories created"

# Clone the repo
echo "-> Cloning repo..."
git clone https://github.com/anchoo2kewl/taskai.git /home/ubuntu/taskai-staging/source 2>&1
echo "OK: Repo cloned at $(cd /home/ubuntu/taskai-staging/source && git log --oneline -1)"

# Generate JWT secret and create .env
JWT_SECRET=$(openssl rand -hex 32)
cat > /home/ubuntu/taskai-staging/.env << EOF
JWT_SECRET=${JWT_SECRET}
EOF
echo "OK: .env created"

# Create docker-compose.yml
cat > /home/ubuntu/taskai-staging/docker-compose.yml << 'COMPOSE'
services:
  api:
    build:
      context: /home/ubuntu/taskai-staging/source/api
      dockerfile: Dockerfile
    ports:
      - "8082:8080"
    container_name: taskai-staging-api
    environment:
      - DB_PATH=/data/taskai.db
      - JWT_SECRET=${JWT_SECRET}
      - PORT=8080
      - ENV=staging
      - CORS_ALLOWED_ORIGINS=https://staging.taskai.cc
      - LOG_LEVEL=${LOG_LEVEL:-info}
      - BREVO_API_KEY=${BREVO_API_KEY:-}
      - CLOUDINARY_CLOUD_NAME=${CLOUDINARY_CLOUD_NAME:-}
      - CLOUDINARY_API_KEY=${CLOUDINARY_API_KEY:-}
      - CLOUDINARY_API_SECRET=${CLOUDINARY_API_SECRET:-}
    volumes:
      - taskai-staging-data:/data
    networks:
      - taskai-staging-network
    restart: unless-stopped
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD", "wget", "--no-verbose", "--tries=1", "--spider", "http://localhost:8080/api/health"]
      interval: 30s
      timeout: 3s
      retries: 3
      start_period: 5s

  web:
    build:
      context: /home/ubuntu/taskai-staging/source
      dockerfile: ./web/Dockerfile
    ports:
      - "8086:80"
    container_name: taskai-staging-web
    networks:
      - taskai-staging-network
    depends_on:
      api:
        condition: service_healthy
    restart: unless-stopped
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD", "curl", "-f", "http://localhost/health"]
      interval: 30s
      timeout: 3s
      retries: 3
      start_period: 5s

  mcp:
    build:
      context: /home/ubuntu/taskai-staging/source/mcp
      dockerfile: Dockerfile
    ports:
      - "8084:3000"
    container_name: taskai-staging-mcp
    environment:
      - TASKAI_API_URL=http://api:8080
      - PORT=3000
    networks:
      - taskai-staging-network
    depends_on:
      api:
        condition: service_healthy
    restart: unless-stopped
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD", "wget", "--no-verbose", "--tries=1", "--spider", "http://localhost:3000/health"]
      interval: 30s
      timeout: 3s
      retries: 3
      start_period: 5s

volumes:
  taskai-staging-data:
    driver: local

networks:
  taskai-staging-network:
    driver: bridge
COMPOSE
echo "OK: docker-compose.yml created"

# Create deploy script
cat > /home/ubuntu/taskai-staging/deploy-app.sh << 'SCRIPT'
#!/bin/bash
set -e
APP_DIR="/home/ubuntu/taskai-staging"
cd "$APP_DIR"
echo "=== Deploying TaskAI (staging) ==="
echo "-> Building Docker images..."
docker compose build --no-cache
echo "-> Stopping old containers..."
docker compose down
echo "-> Starting new containers..."
docker compose up -d
echo "-> Waiting for services to be healthy..."
sleep 10
echo "-> Checking API health..."
for i in {1..30}; do
    if curl -sf http://127.0.0.1:8082/api/health > /dev/null 2>&1; then
        echo "OK: API is healthy"
        break
    fi
    if [ $i -eq 30 ]; then
        echo "FAIL: API health check failed"
        docker compose logs api | tail -20
        exit 1
    fi
    sleep 2
done
echo "-> Checking web health..."
for i in {1..30}; do
    if curl -sf http://127.0.0.1:8086/health > /dev/null 2>&1; then
        echo "OK: Web is healthy"
        break
    fi
    if [ $i -eq 30 ]; then
        echo "FAIL: Web health check failed"
        docker compose logs web | tail -20
        exit 1
    fi
    sleep 2
done
docker image prune -f
echo "=== Deployment completed! ==="
echo "URL: https://staging.taskai.cc"
SCRIPT
chmod +x /home/ubuntu/taskai-staging/deploy-app.sh
echo "OK: deploy script created"
REMOTE_SCRIPT
input.description: Set up staging directory structure on new server

ssh -o ConnectTimeout=10 ubuntu@129.213.82.37 << 'REMOTE_SCRIPT'
set -e

echo "=== Building and starting staging containers ==="
cd /home/ubuntu/taskai-staging
echo "-> Building (this may take a few minutes on ARM)..."
docker compose build --no-cache 2>&1 | tail -10
echo "-> Starting..."
docker compose up -d 2>&1
echo "-> Waiting for health..."
sleep 15

for i in $(seq 1 30); do
    if curl -sf http://127.0.0.1:8082/api/health > /dev/null 2>&1; then
        echo "OK: Staging API is healthy!"
        break
    fi
    if [ $i -eq 30 ]; then
        echo "FAIL: API health check failed after 90s"
        docker compose logs api 2>&1 | tail -30
    fi
    sleep 3
done

echo ""
docker compose ps 2>&1

content.thinking: The nginx on the new server is version 1.24.0 which doesn't support the `http2 on;` directive as a separate line. On older nginx versions, HTTP/2 is enabled via `listen 443 ssl http2;`. Let me fix that.

content.text: Nginx 1.24 doesn't support `http2 on;` as a standalone directive - it needs to be on the `listen` line. Let me fix that.

input.command: ssh -o ConnectTimeout=10 ubuntu@129.213.82.37 "sudo sed -i 's/listen 443 ssl;/listen 443 ssl http2;/g; /http2 on;/d' /etc/nginx/sites-available/staging.taskai.cc && sudo nginx -t 2>&1 && sudo systemctl reload nginx && echo 'OK: nginx fixed and reloaded'"
input.description: Fix http2 directive for nginx 1.24

content.text:
There's already a webhook service on the staging server but it has the old SprintSpark config. Two approaches:

1. Add the prod server's SSH key to staging and update the prod webhook to SSH
2. Update the staging server's own webhook to handle TaskAI staging deploys

Option 2 is cleaner - the staging server handles its own deploys. Let me update the staging webhook config and configure the GitHub Actions to hit the staging server's webhook directly.

input.command:
ssh -o ConnectTimeout=10 ubuntu@129.213.82.37 << 'REMOTE_SCRIPT'
set -e

# Add taskai-staging project to the webhook config
cat > /home/ubuntu/webhooks/config.yaml << 'YAML'
server:
  port: 9876
  host: 0.0.0.0
  log_level: INFO
  log_file: /home/ubuntu/webhooks/logs/webhook.log
defaults:
  secret: default-webhook-secret-2025
  branch: main
  timeout: 30
projects:
  nayantara:
    name: Nayantara's Academic Website
    repository: NayantaraB/academic-website
    secret: nayantara-website-hook-2025
    branch: main
    local_path: /home/ubuntu/projects/nayantara-website
    actions:
    - type: git_pull
    - type: fix_permissions
      command: chmod 644 /home/ubuntu/projects/nayantara-website/*
    - type: custom_command
      command: echo 'Nayantara website updated successfully!'
    enabled: true
  taskai-staging:
    name: TaskAI Staging
    repository: anchoo2kewl/taskai
    secret: taskai-staging-webhook-secret-2025
    branch: main
    local_path: /home/ubuntu/taskai-staging/source
    timeout: 300
    actions:
    - type: git_pull
    - type: custom_command
      command: cd /home/ubuntu/taskai-staging && docker compose build --no-cache
      timeout: 300
    - type: custom_command
      command: cd /home/ubuntu/taskai-staging && docker compose down && docker compose up -d
      timeout: 120
    - type: custom_command
      command: docker image prune -f
    - type: custom_command
      command: echo 'TaskAI staging deployed successfully!'
    enabled: true
  folioworth:
    name: FolioWorth Personal Finance
    repository: anchoo2kewl/folioworth
    secret: 96a55f7ee4df652a34a620558dd0c19f343d3f2f2dfb6892ddec1055203e185b
    branch: main
    local_path: /home/ubuntu/projects/folioworth/source
    timeout: 300
    actions:
    - type: git_pull
    - type: custom_command
      command: cd /home/ubuntu/projects/folioworth && docker compose build --no-cache
      timeout: 300
    - type: custom_command
      command: cd /home/ubuntu/projects/folioworth && docker compose down && docker compose up -d
      timeout: 120
    - type: custom_command
      command: docker image prune -f
    - type: custom_command
      command: echo 'FolioWorth staging deployed successfully!'
    enabled: true
  pingrly:
    name: Pingrly Staging
    repository: anchoo2kewl/pingrly
    secret: bd418003f8700597c44f331abfc5d6b5d65f86bb774bcf5a84a7b2ff95fbe441
    branch: main
    local_path: /home/ubuntu/projects/pingrly/source
    timeout: 300
    actions:
    - type: git_pull
    - type: custom_command
      command: cd /home/ubuntu/projects/pingrly && docker compose -f docker-compose.staging.yml build --no-cache
      timeout: 300
    - type: custom_command
      command: cd /home/ubuntu/projects/pingrly && docker compose -f docker-compose.staging.yml down && docker compose -f docker-compose.staging.yml up -d
      timeout: 120
    - type: custom_command
      command: docker image prune -f
    - type: custom_command
      command: echo Pingrly staging deployed!
    enabled: true
YAML

echo "OK: Webhook config updated"

# Restart the webhook service
cd /home/ubuntu/webhooks
# Check if it's running as a systemd service or python process
if pgrep -f webhook_server.py > /dev/null; then
    echo "-> Restarting webhook server..."
    pkill -f webhook_server.py
    sleep 2
    nohup python3 /home/ubuntu/webhooks/webhook_server.py > /dev/null 2>&1 &
    sleep 2
    echo "OK: Webhook restarted (PID: $(pgrep -f webhook_server.py))"
fi

# Verify
curl -sf http://127.0.0.1:9876/health 2>&1 | python3 -m json.tool 2>/dev/null || echo "Webhook health check"
REMOTE_SCRIPT

content.text: Webhook is up with `taskai-staging` configured. Now the `mcp.staging.taskai.cc` DNS was updated as a CNAME to `staging.taskai.cc`. Let me update the GitHub Actions workflow to hit the staging server's webhook, then get the MCP SSL cert.

input.command: dig +short mcp.staging.taskai.cc @8.8.8.8
input.description: Check mcp.staging DNS resolution

content.content:
staging.taskai.cc.
129.213.82.37

toolUseResult.stdout:
staging.taskai.cc.
129.213.82.37

content.text: Good, `mcp.staging.taskai.cc` now resolves to `129.213.82.37` via CNAME. Let me get the SSL cert and update the GitHub Actions workflow.

input.command:
ssh -o ConnectTimeout=10 ubuntu@129.213.82.37 << 'REMOTE_SCRIPT'
set -e
echo "=== Getting SSL cert for mcp.staging.taskai.cc ==="
sudo certbot --nginx -d mcp.staging.taskai.cc \
    --non-interactive --agree-tos \
    --email anshuman@biswas.me \
    --redirect 2>&1
REMOTE_SCRIPT
input.description: Get SSL cert for mcp.staging.taskai.cc

input.command: ssh -o ConnectTimeout=10 ubuntu@129.213.82.37 "curl -sf https://webhook.biswas.me/health 2>&1 || echo 'Cannot reach webhook.biswas.me from staging'" 2>&1
input.description: Check if staging can reach the webhook endpoint

content.text: Now update the GitHub Actions staging workflow to hit the staging server's webhook instead of the production server's.

content.content:
1→name: Deploy to Staging
     2→
     3→on:
     4→  workflow_run:
     5→    workflows: ["CI"]
     6→    types: [completed]
     7→    branches: [main]
     8→
     9→  # Allow manual trigger from GitHub UI
    10→  workflow_dispatch:
    11→
    12→jobs:
    13→  deploy:
    14→    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    15→    timeout-minutes: 15
    16→    environment: staging
    17→    if: ${{ github.event_name == 'workflow_dispatch' || github.event.workflow_run.conclusion == 'success' }}
    18→
    19→    steps:
    20→      - name: Trigger staging webhook
    21→        run: |
    22→          echo "=== Deploying TaskAI to STAGING ==="
    23→          RESPONSE=$(curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" \
    24→            -X POST "https://webhook.biswas.me/webhook/taskai-staging" \
    25→            -H "X-Hub-Signature-256: sha256=$(echo -n '{}' | openssl dgst -sha256 -hmac '${{ secrets.STAGING_WEBHOOK_SECRET }}' | awk '{print $2}')" \
    26→            -H "X-GitHub-Event: push" \
    27→            -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
    28→            -d '{}')
    29→          echo "Webhook response: $RESPONSE"
    30→          if [ "$RESPONSE" -lt 200 ] || [ "$RESPONSE" -ge 300 ]; then
    31→            echo "WARNING: Webhook returned $RESPONSE, deployment may not have triggered"
    32→          fi
    33→
    34→      - name: Wait for deployment
    35→        run: |
    36→          echo "Waiting 60 seconds for deployment to complete..."
    37→          sleep 60
    38→
    39→      - name: Verify staging health
    40→        run: |
    41→          echo "-> Checking API health..."
    42→          for i in $(seq 1 30); do
    43→            if curl -sf https://staging.taskai.cc/api/health > /dev/null 2>&1; then
    44→              echo "OK: API is healthy"
    45→              break
    46→            fi
    47→            if [ $i -eq 30 ]; then
    48→              echo "FAIL: API health check failed"
    49→              exit 1
    50→            fi
    51→            sleep 5
    52→          done
    53→
    54→          echo "-> Checking web health..."
    55→          for i in $(seq 1 30); do
    56→            if curl -sf -o /dev/null https://staging.taskai.cc/ 2>&1; then
    57→              echo "OK: Web is healthy"
    58→              break
    59→            fi
    60→            if [ $i -eq 30 ]; then
    61→              echo "FAIL: Web health check failed"
    62→              exit 1
    63→            fi
    64→            sleep 5
    65→          done
    66→
    67→          echo "-> Checking MCP health..."
    68→          for i in $(seq 1 10); do
    69→            if curl -sf https://mcp.staging.taskai.cc/health > /dev/null 2>&1; then
    70→              echo "OK: MCP is healthy"
    71→              break
    72→            fi
    73→            if [ $i -eq 10 ]; then
    74→              echo "WARNING: MCP health check failed (non-blocking)"
    75→            fi
    76→            sleep 3
    77→          done
    78→
    79→          echo ""
    80→          echo "=== Staging deployment verified! ==="
    81→          echo "URL: https://staging.taskai.cc"
    82→

file.content:
name: Deploy to Staging

on:
  workflow_run:
    workflows: ["CI"]
    types: [completed]
    branches: [main]

  # Allow manual trigger from GitHub UI
  workflow_dispatch:

jobs:
  deploy:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    timeout-minutes: 15
    environment: staging
    if: ${{ github.event_name == 'workflow_dispatch' || github.event.workflow_run.conclusion == 'success' }}

    steps:
      - name: Trigger staging webhook
        run: |
          echo "=== Deploying TaskAI to STAGING ==="
          RESPONSE=$(curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" \
            -X POST "https://webhook.biswas.me/webhook/taskai-staging" \
            -H "X-Hub-Signature-256: sha256=$(echo -n '{}' | openssl dgst -sha256 -hmac '${{ secrets.STAGING_WEBHOOK_SECRET }}' | awk '{print $2}')" \
            -H "X-GitHub-Event: push" \
            -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
            -d '{}')
          echo "Webhook response: $RESPONSE"
          if [ "$RESPONSE" -lt 200 ] || [ "$RESPONSE" -ge 300 ]; then
            echo "WARNING: Webhook returned $RESPONSE, deployment may not have triggered"
          fi

      - name: Wait for deployment
        run: |
          echo "Waiting 60 seconds for deployment to complete..."
          sleep 60

      - name: Verify staging health
        run: |
          echo "-> Checking API health..."
          for i in $(seq 1 30); do
            if curl -sf https://staging.taskai.cc/api/health > /dev/null 2>&1; then
              echo "OK: API is healthy"
              break
            fi
            if [ $i -eq 30 ]; then
              echo "FAIL: API health check failed"
              exit 1
            fi
            sleep 5
          done

          echo "-> Checking web health..."
          for i in $(seq 1 30); do
            if curl -sf -o /dev/null https://staging.taskai.cc/ 2>&1; then
              echo "OK: Web is healthy"
              break
            fi
            if [ $i -eq 30 ]; then
              echo "FAIL: Web health check failed"
              exit 1
            fi
            sleep 5
          done

          echo "-> Checking MCP health..."
          for i in $(seq 1 10); do
            if curl -sf https://mcp.staging.taskai.cc/health > /dev/null 2>&1; then
              echo "OK: MCP is healthy"
              break
            fi
            if [ $i -eq 10 ]; then
              echo "WARNING: MCP health check failed (non-blocking)"
            fi
            sleep 3
          done

          echo ""
          echo "=== Staging deployment verified! ==="
          echo "URL: https://staging.taskai.cc"

name: Deploy to Production

on:
  workflow_dispatch:
    inputs:
      ref:
        description: 'Git ref to deploy'
        required: false
        default: 'main'

jobs:
  deploy:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    timeout-minutes: 15
    environment: production

    steps:
      - name: Display deployment info
        run: |
          echo "Deploying to PRODUCTION"
          echo "Ref: ${{ github.event.inputs.ref }}"
          echo "Triggered by: ${{ github.actor }}"

      - name: Trigger production webhook
        run: |
          echo "=== Deploying TaskAI to PRODUCTION ==="
          RESPONSE=$(curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" \
            -X POST "https://webhook.biswas.me/webhook/taskai" \
            -H "X-Hub-Signature-256: sha256=$(echo -n '{}' | openssl dgst -sha256 -hmac '${{ secrets.PRODUCTION_WEBHOOK_SECRET }}' | awk '{print $2}')" \
            -H "X-GitHub-Event: push" \
            -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
            -d '{}')
          echo "Webhook response: $RESPONSE"
          if [ "$RESPONSE" -lt 200 ] || [ "$RESPONSE" -ge 300 ]; then
            echo "WARNING: Webhook returned $RESPONSE, deployment may not have triggered"
          fi

      - name: Wait for deployment
        run: |
          echo "Waiting 60 seconds for deployment to complete..."
          sleep 60

      - name: Verify production health
        run: |
          echo "-> Checking API health..."
          for i in $(seq 1 30); do
            if curl -sf https://taskai.cc/api/health > /dev/null 2>&1; then
              echo "OK: API is healthy"
              break
            fi
            if [ $i -eq 30 ]; then
              echo "FAIL: API health check failed"
              exit 1
            fi
            sleep 5
          done

          echo "-> Checking web health..."
          for i in $(seq 1 30); do
            if curl -sf -o /dev/null https://taskai.cc/ 2>&1; then
              echo "OK: Web is healthy"
              break
            fi
            if [ $i -eq 30 ]; then
              echo "FAIL: Web health check failed"
              exit 1
            fi
            sleep 5
          done

          echo "-> Checking MCP health..."
          for i in $(seq 1 10); do
            if curl -sf https://mcp.taskai.cc/health > /dev/null 2>&1; then
              echo "OK: MCP is healthy"
              break
            fi
            if [ $i -eq 10 ]; then
              echo "WARNING: MCP health check failed (non-blocking)"
            fi
            sleep 3
          done

          echo ""
          echo "=== Production deployment completed! ==="
          echo "URL: https://taskai.cc"

content.thinking:
The production deploy also hits `webhook.biswas.me/webhook/taskai` - but `webhook.biswas.me` points to the staging server (`129.213.82.37`), and the staging server's webhook config doesn't have a `taskai` (production) project. The production webhook is on `31.97.102.48`.

We have a few options:
1. Add a `taskai` project to the staging webhook that SSHes into the production server
2. Change the production workflow to hit the production server's webhook directly (not via `webhook.biswas.me`)
3. Set up a separate webhook domain for production


4. Route through `taskai.cc` with nginx proxying to the webhook service, or add a separate webhook domain on the production server itself

The cleanest solution is probably using `taskai.cc` to reach the production webhook through nginx, since the IP+port approach won't work well with HTTPS.

content.text: Problem: both staging and production deploy workflows hit `webhook.biswas.me` which now points to the staging server. The production webhook on `31.97.102.48` is unreachable via that domain. Let me fix the production deploy to route through a different path.

content.thinking:
The production server has a webhook running on port 9876 with the `taskai` project configured. But there's no nginx config routing to it via a domain. The `taskai.cc` nginx config proxies API and web, not the webhook.

The simplest solution: add a webhook location to the existing `taskai.cc` nginx config, or create a `webhook.taskai.cc` domain. But creating a new domain/subdomain would require DNS + SSL.

Simpler approach: add a `/webhook/` location to the `taskai.cc` nginx config that proxies to the local webhook service on port 9876. Then update the production deploy workflow to use `https://taskai.cc/webhook/taskai` instead of `https://webhook.biswas.me/webhook/taskai`.


I'll implement that now.

content.text: The production webhook is running on `31.97.102.48:9876`. I'll add a `/webhook/` route to the `taskai.cc` nginx config and update the GitHub Actions workflow.

input.command: ssh -o ConnectTimeout=10 ubuntu@31.97.102.48 "cat /etc/nginx/sites-available/taskai.cc" 2>&1
input.description: Read current taskai.cc nginx config

input.command:
ssh -o ConnectTimeout=10 ubuntu@31.97.102.48 << 'REMOTE_SCRIPT'
set -e

# Add webhook location to taskai.cc nginx config (before the frontend catch-all)
sudo sed -i '/# Frontend (React app via web container)/i\
    # Webhook endpoint for CI/CD deploys\
    location /webhook/ {\
        proxy_pass http://127.0.0.1:9876;\
        proxy_http_version 1.1;\
        proxy_set_header Host $host;\
        proxy_set_header X-Real-IP $remote_addr;\
        proxy_set_header X-Forwarded-For $proxy_add_x_forwarded_for;\
        proxy_set_header X-Forwarded-Proto $scheme;\
        proxy_connect_timeout 300s;\
        proxy_send_timeout 300s;\
        proxy_read_timeout 300s;\
    }\
' /etc/nginx/sites-available/taskai.cc

sudo nginx -t 2>&1 && sudo systemctl reload nginx
echo "OK: Webhook route added to taskai.cc"

# Test it
curl -sf -o /dev/null -w "HTTP %{http_code}" https://taskai.cc/webhook/taskai -X POST -H "Content-Type: application/json" -d '{}' 2>&1
echo ""
REMOTE_SCRIPT
input.description: Add webhook route to taskai.cc nginx

#!/bin/bash

# TaskAI Server Management Script
# Manages deployment, testing, health checks, and operations

set -e

# Colors for output
RED='\033[0;31m'
GREEN='\033[0;32m'
YELLOW='\033[1;33m'
BLUE='\033[0;34m'
PURPLE='\033[0;35m'
CYAN='\033[0;36m'
NC='\033[0m' # No Color

# Server connection
REMOTE_HOST="31.97.102.48"
REMOTE_USER="ubuntu"
REMOTE_SERVER="${REMOTE_USER}@${REMOTE_HOST}"

# Staging environment
STAGING_DEPLOY_PATH="/home/ubuntu/taskai-staging"
STAGING_DOMAIN="staging.taskai.cc"
STAGING_API_PORT=8082

            REMOTE_API_PORT="$PROD_API_PORT"
            REMOTE_WEB_PORT="$PROD_WEB_PORT"
            REMOTE_MCP_PORT="$PROD_MCP_PORT"
            REMOTE_MCP_DOMAIN="$PROD_MCP_DOMAIN"

            REMOTE_WEB_PORT="$STAGING_WEB_PORT"
            REMOTE_MCP_PORT="$STAGING_MCP_PORT"
            REMOTE_MCP_DOMAIN="$STAGING_MCP_DOMAIN"
            ENV_LABEL="STAGING"
            ;;
    esac
}
